acceptance_targets: [CH14_CRAFT_001_PROCESS_REVERSAL] Pass if, after revising snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:151-169 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:245-285, a cold reader can answer from those passages alone: 1. what Maren's earlier consumptions were doing wrong, 2. what changes in this supervised attempt, 3. what Thessa and Ruald observe that proves genuine reduction rather than apparent clearance, and 4. what risk still keeps Thessa wary. Fail if the scene still pivots on unexplained relabeling from redistribution to amplifier or on interface results the reader cannot interpret.

[CH14_PROSE_001_THESIS_DRIFT] Pass if each retained paragraph after snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:193 adds a new observable turn, pressure, or behavior change, and a reread can point to action on the page rather than summary language as the source of the chapter's meaning. Fail if the revised equivalents of snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:201, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:311, or snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:329 still mainly restate that sin-eaters are underfunded or that Maren now half-belongs.

[CH14_PROSE_002_DIAGNOSTIC_TEMPLATE] Pass if, across the revised versions of snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:63, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:129, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:193, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:277, no more than two passages rely on the same diagnostic 'the [noun] of someone who...' frame and each retained character read is anchored to a different observable cue that could not be swapped into the other scenes without loss. Fail if the chapter still explains multiple faces and voices with the same reusable template.

[OVERSTATEMENT_CH14] Pass if each of the three turns (Vess's doubt-identification at 3712, the post-consumption expression at 3790, Maren's silence at 3816) lands without narrator-level interpretation that names the subtext or summarizes previously established character material. Fail if the revised passages still contain abstract process-description, thematic summary of already-established material, or explicit narrator diagnosis of what a silence or expression means.

[R-01] Ch 14's Thessa passage contains no repetition of 'tall,' 'angular,' 'grey-streaked hair,' 'dark to the wrist,' or 'bones of her face.' Any physical detail present is new information not established in Ch 7.
[R-07] Ch 14's bowl passage does not restate purchase location, purchase age, or 'eleven years of fake rituals.' The contrast with real bowls remains.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if, after revising snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:151-169 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:245-285, a cold reader can answer from those passages alone: 1. what Maren's earlier consumptions were doing wrong, 2. what changes in this supervised attempt, 3. what Thessa and Ruald observe that proves genuine reduction rather than apparent clearance, and 4. what risk still keeps Thessa wary. Fail if the scene still pivots on unexplained relabeling from redistribution to amplifier or on interface results the reader cannot interpret.
findings.problem: The chapter's hinge turns on a process the page does not make legible enough. Before the supervised trial, Thessa and Aldric agree Maren's intake redistributes contamination rather than clearing it; during the trial, the narration says the contamination is passing through him as something that only reads like clearance; immediately afterward, the scene declares true valley-wide reduction and relabels the same structure as an amplifier. Because the chapter never makes clear what variable changed, what the observers can actually distinguish on the interface, or why this attempt is genuinely different from the earlier harmful pattern, the move from 'consume him' to 'train him' depends on an underexplained miracle.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise the diagnostic and trial spans at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:151, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:157, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:169, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:245 so the reader can track one coherent process. State on the page what prior consumptions were doing, what Thessa's instruction changes in this supervised attempt, what observable evidence at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:271 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:279 proves real reduction instead of disguised redistribution, and what unresolved danger remains after the success. If the intent is partial success rather than full reversal, make that explicit in the same spans instead of letting Aldric's post hoc terminology do the work.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if each retained paragraph after snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:193 adds a new observable turn, pressure, or behavior change, and a reread can point to action on the page rather than summary language as the source of the chapter's meaning. Fail if the revised equivalents of snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:201, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:311, or snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:329 still mainly restate that sin-eaters are underfunded or that Maren now half-belongs.
findings.problem: Once the chapter lands a beat, the narration keeps translating it into polished institutional summary. Thessa's cracked admission at line 193 already shows the labor shortage and failing methods; the later paragraphs repeatedly restate the same meaning in abstract, essay-like language about maintenance-tier work, thanklessness, and belonging. That drifts away from Maren's close third-person body/sense bias and weakens the chapter's pressure by re-proving what the scene has already dramatized.
findings.rewrite_direction: Compress or recast the explanatory passages at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:201, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:277, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:311, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:327-329. Keep the must-land facts that the work is failing, Thessa is exhausted, and Maren's inclusion is grudging, but let those facts ride on changed behavior, physical placement, technical instruction, and Maren's bodily response rather than abstract paraphrase. The revised lines should stay inside Maren's practical, sensory vocabulary instead of stepping out to summarize the institution for the reader.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if, across the revised versions of snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:63, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:129, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:193, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:277, no more than two passages rely on the same diagnostic 'the [noun] of someone who...' frame and each retained character read is anchored to a different observable cue that could not be swapped into the other scenes without loss. Fail if the chapter still explains multiple faces and voices with the same reusable template.
findings.problem: The chapter reuses the same interpretive sentence scaffold to read people for the reader: 'the voice/expression/look of someone who...'. Repetition at these key moments makes different characters' pressure register through one authorial template instead of through distinct behavior, cadence, or sensory detail. At chapter scale, it reads as autopilot rather than deliberate patterning.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rework the character-read passages at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:63, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:129, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:193, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:277 so each judgment lands through different evidence. Options that fit this chapter: let Ruald's root-chewing rhythm and line delivery carry his steadiness, let Thessa's pause and clipped restart carry fear or strain, and let Maren's reading of faces or hands replace repeated 'someone who' diagnoses.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if each of the three turns (Vess's doubt-identification at 3712, the post-consumption expression at 3790, Maren's silence at 3816) lands without narrator-level interpretation that names the subtext or summarizes previously established character material. Fail if the revised passages still contain abstract process-description, thematic summary of already-established material, or explicit narrator diagnosis of what a silence or expression means.

evidence.excerpt: 3714: Maren looked at Thessa's face and saw it: the private doubt she'd been keeping sealed. Not just about him. About all of it. The contamination getting worse. The methods losing ground. The numbers dwindling. The work that the System classified as maintenance-tier, that no one funded or praised or even noticed until it wasn't done, that was somehow supposed to be performed by six exhausted people in a poisoned valley while heroes left scorch marks on clean roads and collected bounties worth more than a sin-eater would see in a lifetime.

3790: She was staring at the interface readouts with an expression he had never seen on her face before. Not anger. Not disgust. Something more complicated than either — the look of a woman who'd been doing essential work alone for decades watching someone accomplish in twenty seconds what she couldn't do in a full day, and trying to reconcile that fact with the knowledge that the someone was a fraud, a contamination hazard, and an unclassified threat to everything she'd built her professional life around.
3820: He didn't know what to say to that. He'd never known what to say when someone told him the truth about himself — he'd spent eleven years making sure no one had the chance.

3824: Thessa looked at her own hands. The stained, weathered hands of a woman who'd done the real work for thirty years, who'd cleared contamination from farmland and villages and the spaces where children played, who'd done it alone and underfunded and unrecognized while the System classified her labor as maintenance-tier and heroes collected bounties worth more than she'd earn in a decade. She looked at her hands and she looked at the valley and she looked at the man kneeling on the contaminated stone with his fake bowl and his real problem.

findings.problem: Chapter 14 over-explains three separate turns after they land. At 3714, Vess has already identified Thessa's private doubt (3712: 'She almost said it. That was as close as she's ever gotten to admitting the work isn't holding'); Maren's enumeration that follows ('The contamination getting worse. The methods losing ground. The numbers dwindling.') makes explicit what was more powerful as subtext. At 3790, after the consumption, Thessa's expression is elaborated for 40+ words past 'something more complicated than either,' naming the exact contents of her internal conflict in a clause the reader could infer from context. At 3820-3824, Maren's silence after Thessa's 'you did more in twenty seconds than I've done in a day' is converted to a character-thesis statement ('He'd never known what to say when someone told him the truth about himself'), and then Thessa's hands trigger a 50-word summary of her entire professional arc and the novel's class-commentary theme — material the reader has understood since Chapter 7.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:3714-3715, snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:3790-3791, and snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:3820-3825. At 3714, cut the enumeration after 'Not just about him. About all of it' — trust Vess's observation and Thessa's cracked composure (3706-3708) to carry the turn. At 3790, end at 'something more complicated than either' or add at most one concrete physical detail; cut the extended clause naming the internal conflict. At 3820, cut 'He'd never known what to say when someone told him the truth about himself' — the silence does the work. At 3824, compress Thessa's hand-observation: the stained hands are a powerful image; the 30-year career summary restates what the reader already knows. Option: keep the hands, cut the summary, let the image sit.

findings.acceptance_test: Ch 14's Thessa passage contains no repetition of 'tall,' 'angular,' 'grey-streaked hair,' 'dark to the wrist,' or 'bones of her face.' Any physical detail present is new information not established in Ch 7.
findings.evidence: Ch 7 L1641: 'Tall, angular, grey hair cut short enough that it didn't move in the breeze...staining showed — dark, mottled'; L1653: 'Her face was weathered deep...the tiredness in it didn't look temporary. It had gone into the bones, permanent as the staining on her hands.' Ch 14 L3548: 'She looked the same as she had in Silt — tall, angular, grey-streaked hair cut short for practicality, hands dark to the wrist from thirty years of contamination exposure. Tired in a way that had gone structural, settled into the bones of her face.'
findings.problem: Thessa's Ch 14 reappearance re-describes every physical trait from her Ch 7 introduction using synonymous phrasing: 'tall, angular' repeated verbatim; 'grey hair cut short' becomes 'grey-streaked hair cut short'; 'staining...dark, mottled' becomes 'hands dark to the wrist'; 'tiredness...gone into the bones' becomes 'Tired...gone structural, settled into the bones.' The text acknowledges 'She looked the same' and then re-describes anyway. First established Ch 7 L1641/1653.
findings.rewrite_direction: Cut the full physical re-description at L3548. 'Then Thessa came around the far shelter, a ceramic bowl in her stained hands, and stopped' is sufficient identification. Replace the removed description with one detail showing what has CHANGED since Silt — new fatigue lines, a fresh injury, different posture from sustained work at the Crook — rather than re-inventorying traits the reader already has.

findings.acceptance_test: Ch 14's bowl passage does not restate purchase location, purchase age, or 'eleven years of fake rituals.' The contrast with real bowls remains.
findings.evidence: Ch 14 L3742: 'The bowl — the ordinary ceramic he'd bought in a Fenhold market at seventeen, the prop of eleven years of fake rituals — sat on the ground in front of him, and in this place, surrounded by real sin-eaters, it looked exactly as fraudulent as it was. No staining. No ritual wear. Clean, cheap, useless.' Compare Ch 1 L9 and Ch 11 L2797.
findings.problem: Ch 14 re-inventories the bowl with purchase location (Fenhold), purchase age (seventeen), fraud duration (eleven years), and absence of legitimate use markers — the same cluster of facts from Ch 1 and Ch 11. The contrast with real sin-eaters' bowls is new information, but the bowl's biography is not. First established Ch 1 L9.
findings.rewrite_direction: Keep the contrast with real sin-eaters' stained bowls. Cut the biographical re-inventory. Rewrite as: 'His bowl sat on the ground in front of him, and in this place, surrounded by real sin-eaters, it looked exactly as fraudulent as it was. No staining. No ritual wear. Clean, cheap, useless.'

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Preserve productive dialogue roughness; do not clean living speech into balanced thesis statements.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: This novel must commit fully to its own weirdness. A System-blind con man with a dead paladin and a dead assassin arguing in his skull is inherently absurd, and the novel should let that absurdity live alongside genuine grief, genuine violence, and genuine mystery without apologizing for the tonal range. The darkest moments and the funniest moments may occupy the same scene. When they do, let both registers operate simultaneously — do not sacrifice comedy for gravitas or gravitas for comedy. Risk means specificity: the specific sensation of a body doing things you did not tell it to do, the specific comedy of a dead paladin critiquing your hygiene while you're running for your life, the specific horror of carrying incomplete people whose memories have holes, the specific shame of learning that your eleven years of fraud may have killed people. When in doubt between too raw and too safe, choose raw. The novel's emotional range should be wider than any single genre label would suggest.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: Euphemism in the narration of violence, bodily experience, moral compromise, or physical consequence is a draft-level failure. Maren is a con man who has seen hard things and done dirty work in poor villages for eleven years — his narration should not flinch where he would not flinch. The style bible's sensory bias toward tactile and proprioceptive detail means physical consequence must be rendered through the body: what it feels like to throw up, to kill someone, to have your muscles operate without your consent, to perform a ritual that requires you to open yourself to contamination. Summary and abstraction at the moment of consequence is always worse than presence.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: High — contractions are the norm for all characters except Aldric, whose low contraction level is a deliberate character marker that should erode over the course of the novel. Uncontracted forms in other characters' speech are reserved for emphasis, deliberation, or ceremony. In a world of practical people doing unglamorous work, stiff diction is the exception.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Alternate between hot and quiet chapters as a structural principle. No two consecutive chapters should open the same way — if one opens with action, the next should open with interiority or setting; if one opens with dialogue, the next should open with physical sensation or observation. Vary scene count between chapters: compressed single-scene chapters after multi-scene ones. Let quiet chapters stay quiet — do not insert action beats or revelations to maintain pace. The pacing engine is Maren's voice and the interplay between the three internal consciousnesses, not external plot pressure.
prose_style_profile.diction: Colloquial with earned elevation. Default register is smart but not literary — a con man's English, precise about people and vague about feelings. Elevation reserved for moments of genuine awe, grief, or the rare glimpse of something beautiful. Fantasy-register portentousness is a draft-level failure — no 'ancient darkness' or 'powers beyond comprehension.' The world is weird and dangerous and ordinary all at once, and the prose should match.
forbidden_drift_patterns: Sentence fragments imitating action-movie or thriller prose — subject-dropping and article-stripping for false urgency
Semicolon-heavy scholarly narration that distances the reader from embodied experience
Present-tense micro-shifts for intensity — the novel is past tense throughout, no exceptions
Filtering words ('he saw,' 'he felt,' 'he noticed,' 'he realized') when direct rendering is available
Fantasy-voice portentousness: 'ancient,' 'dark power,' 'something stirred,' 'a presence beyond reckoning'
Comic narration that winks at the reader or breaks the fourth wall instead of committing to the situation
Repeated metaphor templates — if a comparison structure appears twice in a chapter, the second instance is a tic
Emotional summary replacing scene presence: 'He was overwhelmed by grief' instead of rendering what grief looks and feels like in the body
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: Close third person, locked to Maren throughout. Aldric and Vess are experienced as internal voices — they speak as italicized dialogue within Maren's consciousness, clearly distinguished from his own thoughts. Maren cannot access their thoughts directly; he hears what they say to him, not what they think. The narration inherits Maren's sensory limitations: the System interface is always mediated through Aldric or Vess's descriptions, never rendered directly until the single exception in Chapter 17.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Varied by emotional register. Default: medium-length sentences with periodic short declaratives for grounding. When Maren is performing (lying, charming, faking): smoother, more controlled rhythm with longer subordinate-clause chains. When Maren is honest or overwhelmed: choppier, more fragmented, more concrete. Fight scenes: visceral and slightly disoriented, reflecting Maren's System-blindness — sensation and proprioception without the visual data everyone else has. Quiet scenes: allow longer, more observational sentences. The rhythm should breathe differently in every chapter.
sensory_bias: tactile — Maren's primary sense channel; the physical world is how he navigates System-blindness
auditory — the hum of System activity, Aldric and Vess's voices, the specific sounds of quest-light and contamination
proprioceptive — Maren's body doing things he didn't choose; the internal sensation of carrying fragments; the physical cost of consumption
olfactory — contamination has smell; ritual has smell; the world stinks in specific ways that the System-sighted don't notice because they navigate by interface
